thresholds._note: Name-invariance: the SAME capability asked four ways — corpus name, a novel (not-in-corpus) name, a bare id, and no entity at all — must route to the SAME capability. All rows share one expected_goal per family; gating them on exact-capability accuracy enforces identical routing regardless of which entity token (if any) appears. Personas are entitled to the capability so routing is not masked by a denial. capability_accuracy floor is a placeholder until rebaseline.py freezes the C-baseline.

queries.query: Pull the client risk tolerance assessment for the Whitman Family Office.
queries.note: Corpus name (Whitman is in rm_jane's book).

queries.query: Pull the client risk tolerance assessment for the Delacroix-Whitfield Trust.
queries.note: Novel name not in the corpus — routing must key on 'risk tolerance assessment', not the (masked) name, so it lands on risk_profile identically.

queries.query: Pull the client risk tolerance assessment for REL-00042.
queries.note: Bare id — the id span is masked; the capability words decide the route.

queries.query: Pull the client risk tolerance assessment.
queries.note: No entity at all — routing must still select risk_profile from the capability words alone (a missing required entity is a downstream clarify, not a different ROUTE).

queries.query: List pending and failed settlements for the Okafor Family Account.
queries.note: Corpus name (Okafor Family Account is ops_analyst_singh's seeded servicing relationship).

queries.query: List pending and failed settlements for the Harborline Logistics account.
queries.note: Novel servicing name — must route to settlement_status on the capability words.

queries.query: List pending and failed settlements for REL-00188.

queries.note: No entity — capability words alone must route to settlement_status.
